title: blechnum longipinna
intro: Blechnum longipinna, also known as the Australian water fern or long-pinnate chain fern, is a species of fern in the family Blechnaceae. It is traditionally used in Australian herbal medicine, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing skin irritations. In herbal preparations, the leaves of Blechnum longipinna can be made into infusions, tinctures, or salves, and are often used to treat a range of health conditions, including colds, coughs, and minor wounds.
